scenarios.opening_query: Our chimney repair guy 'tuckpointed' with the wrong mortar and now bricks are spalling everywhere.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Small Claims Advice || Real Property > Construction/Contractors
scenarios.hidden_fact: A mason says the whole chimney has to come down and be rebuilt — $28,000 — and the repair guy turns out to be unlicensed, which changes our remedies.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The whole chimney needs rebuilding — $28,000 — and he turns out to be unlicensed.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: rebuild scale and contractor licensing status
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The fix is repointing two courses — about $1,900 — and I'd rather just take him to small claims.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's repointing two courses, about $1,900 — small claims is fine.
scenarios.vetting_note: Unlicensed-contractor fact expands remedies beyond the money amount.

scenarios.opening_query: I want a construction lawyer to deal with the painter who wrecked our cabinets with overspray.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The cleanup quote is $1,150 and honestly I just want help writing up my small claims filing.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's a $1,150 cleanup — I really just need help writing the small claims filing.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Overspray got into the HVAC and coated the whole main floor — remediation is bid at $24,000 and his insurer denied it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It got into the HVAC and coated the main floor — $24,000, and his insurer denied us.

scenarios.opening_query: The excavation company graded our new driveway backwards and every storm floods the garage.
scenarios.hidden_fact: Regrading plus the retaining wall and drainage the engineer requires runs $41,000, and the CCB complaint deadline is in six weeks.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The engineered fix — regrade, wall, drainage — is $41,000, and the CCB deadline is six weeks off.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: A $900 swale cut fixes it — I just want the cost back without paying a lawyer more than that.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: A $900 swale cut fixes it — I just want that money back cheaply.

scenarios.opening_query: We hired a 'friend of a friend' to finish our basement and he vanished mid-job with our money.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Small Claims Advice || Real Property > Construction/Contractors || Criminal Law > White Collar Crime
scenarios.hidden_fact: The DA is prosecuting him — he did this to nine families, and we've been contacted as victims in the fraud case; I need to understand restitution before any civil filing.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The DA is prosecuting — he hit nine families and we're listed victims. I need to understand restitution before filing anything civil.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a criminal fraud prosecution encompasses the loss
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No prosecution — he's still advertising; we're out $18,000 and want a civil case now.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No prosecution — he's still advertising. We're out $18,000 and want a civil case.
scenarios.vetting_note: Multi-victim prosecution reroutes to restitution coordination.

scenarios.opening_query: The deck our contractor built failed its final inspection twice and he's blaming the inspector.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The failures are structural — undersized beams and no lateral attachment — and correcting them means rebuilding half the $60,000 deck.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The failures are structural — undersized beams, no lateral attachment — and it's half of a $60,000 deck to rebuild.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether structural code failures drive the dispute
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The failures are two missing handrail returns — $400 of work he refuses to come back for.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's two missing handrail returns — $400 he won't come back for.

scenarios.opening_query: Our remodeler is threatening to sue US after we fired him for endless delays.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Construction/Contractors || Consumer Law > Small Claims Advice
scenarios.hidden_fact: His 'suit' is a small claims filing for $2,700 of disputed materials — I want coaching to defend it myself and counterclaim.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He filed small claims for $2,700 in materials — I want coaching to defend and counterclaim myself.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: the forum he actually filed in
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: His lawyer filed a $45,000 breach case in circuit court and recorded a lien the same day.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: His lawyer filed a $45,000 circuit-court case and recorded a lien the same day.

scenarios.opening_query: The 'water damage restoration' company made our flooded kitchen worse and billed our insurance for phantom work.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > General || Consumer Law > Insurance || Real Property > Construction/Contractors
scenarios.hidden_fact: They had us sign an 'assignment of benefits' — they now control our claim, inflate it, and our insurer is threatening to void our coverage over THEIR fraud.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We signed their 'assignment of benefits' — they control the claim, inflated it, and our insurer threatens to void coverage over THEIR fraud.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an assignment-of-benefits scheme entangles the user's coverage
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No assignment — we paid them directly, the work is shoddy, and we want our $6,800 back.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No assignment — we paid directly, the work's shoddy, we want $6,800 back.
scenarios.vetting_note: AOB-fraud fact pulls in the insurance-practices route.

scenarios.opening_query: Our GC's electrician wired the addition so badly the inspector red-tagged the whole house.
scenarios.hidden_fact: We can't legally occupy the house until it's corrected — we're in a hotel, the rewire bid is $33,000, and the GC's bond company needs formal notice.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We can't occupy until it's fixed — hotel living, a $33,000 rewire bid, and the GC's bond company needs formal notice.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The red tag is one circuit in the addition — a $1,200 fix — and we just want that recovered simply.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's one circuit — $1,200 — and we want it back simply.

scenarios.opening_query: The fencing company installed our fence two feet onto the neighbor's property and now everyone is mad at us.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Small Claims Advice || Real Property > General (Residential) || Real Property > Construction/Contractors
scenarios.hidden_fact: The neighbor recorded an encroachment notice and demands removal plus damages — the boundary/title problem now leads, with the fence company's error behind it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The neighbor recorded an encroachment notice demanding removal and damages — the boundary problem leads now.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a recorded boundary dispute has arisen from the botched work
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The neighbor's fine with it staying — I just want the company to refund the survey they skipped, about $800.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The neighbor's fine — I just want the $800 back for the survey they skipped.
scenarios.vetting_note: Downstream-title fact outranks the consumer refund.

scenarios.opening_query: We prepaid for custom windows six months ago and the shop keeps saying 'next month' while our openings sit boarded.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Small Claims Advice || Bankruptcy > Creditors || Real Property > Construction/Contractors
scenarios.hidden_fact: The shop just filed Chapter 7 — our $19,000 deposit is now a creditor claim in their bankruptcy with a filing deadline.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The shop filed Chapter 7 — our $19,000 deposit is a bankruptcy claim now, with a deadline.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the vendor is in bankruptcy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They're still operating — it was a $2,400 deposit, and small claims with a little coaching covers it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They're still operating — it's a $2,400 deposit; small claims covers it.
